title: Wormhole
description:
"In 1927 when people had occasion to ask me about my prognostications and I told them what I thought it would be appropriate to do about what I could see ahead for the 1950'S, 1960’s, and 1970’s people used to say to me, “Very amusing — you are a thousand years ahead of your time.” Having myself studied the increments in which we can think forwardly I was amazed at the ease with which the rest of society seemed to be able to see a thousand years ahead while I could see only one-fortieth of that time distance. As time went on people began to tell me that I was a hundred years ahead, and now they tell me that I’m a little behind the times."
- R. Buckminster Fuller, "Operating Manual for Spaceship Earth"

"Thirty spokes converge upon a single hub
It is the hole in the centre that the purpose of the axle depends.
We make a vessel from a lump of clay
It is the empty space within the vessel that makes it useful.
We make doors and windows for a room.
But it is the those empty spaces that make the room habitable.
Thus while the tangible has advantages
It is the intangible that makes it useful."
- Laozi, Tao Te Ching (ca. late 4th cent. BC)
